purpose: Cal-Access donor name alias-merge map. Used by scripts/ingest-cal-access-bulk.cjs to collapse spelling variants into one canonical name before edge aggregation. Per audit Remediation #8 (cc_p3_191 Finding 3.4).
format: Each entry maps an array of variant strings to a canonical_name. The ingester's normalizeDonorName output is matched (case-insensitively) against the variants array; if a hit, the canonical_name is used as the donor name on the edge.
matching: Variants compared case-insensitively, whitespace-collapsed, against the post-normalization donor name. Exact match only — substring matching invites false positives (e.g. 'PG&E' inside 'PG&E Lobbyists' is a different entity).
review: Code Claude proposes additions when discovery surfaces a new spelling variant cluster. David batch-approves via the editorial-decision-pipeline (Tier 2 per ADR-0029). Direct edits here require a [pipeline] tag in the commit and explicit reasoning.

aliases.rationale: PG&E (utility holding company) — multiple Cal-Access spelling variants. Audit cc_p3_191 surfaced 3 variants in the data: 'Pg&e Corporation' (post-normalize lowercase), 'PG&E Corporation' (mixed-case input), 'PG&E' (alone).
AT&T — telecom donor with similar acronym normalization issues as PG&E.

variants: service employees international union california state council
seiu ca state council for working people
seiu california state council for working people
aliases.rationale: SEIU California State Council — major IE donor (Swalwell IE PAC); long-form vs short-form vs 'For Working People' suffix all observed in the data.
